message:
Add true async support for HTTP and spawn operations

Implements poll-based async architecture that enables non-blocking I/O:

HTTP Async API (edgebox_http module):
- start_async: Starts HTTP request, returns request ID immediately
- poll: Checks if request complete (0=pending, 1=done, <0=error)
- async_response_len/async_response: Get response when done
- async_free: Clean up request

Spawn Async API (edgebox_spawn module):
- start: Spawns child process, returns spawn ID immediately
- poll: Checks if process complete
- output_len/output: Get stdout/stderr/exitCode when done
- free: Clean up spawn

JavaScript Integration:
- fetch() now uses async API with setTimeout polling
- child_process.spawn() now uses async API with setTimeout polling
- Both yield control between polls allowing other JS to run

This enables Promise.all([fetch1, fetch2, spawn1]) to work properly,
with all operations running concurrently.

Sandbox Safety:
- All operations still go through host bridge
- Host enforces .edgebox.json permissions
- WASM can only poll/query, cannot bypass security
